text: The patient was positioned in the left lateral decubitus flex position and intubated with a double lumen endotracheal tube allowing collapse of the right lung.
5mm ports were placed abdominally with a 12mm umbilical port.
The right hemiliver was mobilised from the seen adhesions here.
The right hepatic vein was subsequently exposed after freeing of the right triangular ligament.
The lesion was demarcated using laparoscopic ultrasound and hook cautery.
Further dissection proceeded to expose the right hepatic vein.
Parenchymal transection proceeds using ligature device anteriorly from an abdominal approach initially as seen here.
Copious irrigation is applied to prevent the ligature from sticking.
At this point the intercostal ports were placed through the chest and a view of the lungs and heart are seen and other intercostal ports are placed under direct vision.
Here with the diaphragm being pressed up against the lateral chest wall
and 5mm ports placed with cuffs inflated.
Parenchymal transection proceeds anteriorly as able and once becoming too difficult to access switch to intercostal ports to continue the posterior aspect of the transection.
Prior to transection a pringle loop was placed for vascular control if need be.
As vessels are encountered a haemlock clip is placed and vessels transected with the ligature.
As can be seen here damage occurred
to the right hepatic vein.
Control was gained by compression with the sucker and application of hemlock clips also assisted by increased CO2 pneumoperitoneum pressures up to 20 mmHg.
Transdiaphragmatic ports were removed and repaired to the diaphragm using 3-0 Novofil sutures.
Operative time was 140 minutes and total blood loss 250 mls.
The specimen was removed via a 12mm endocatch taken through the umbilical port.
